2605.02411.title: FitText: Evolving Agent Tool Ecologies via Memetic Retrieval
2605.02411.paper_summary: A semantic gap separates how users describe tasks from how tools are documented. As API ecosystems scale to tens of thousands of endpoints, static retrieval from the initial query alone cannot bridge this gap: the agent's understanding of what it needs evolves during execution, but its tool set does not. We introduce FitText, a training-free framework that makes retrieval dynamic by embedding it directly in the agent's reasoning loop. FitText generates natural-language pseudo-tool descriptions as retrieval probes, refines them iteratively using retrieval feedback, and explores diverse alternatives through stochastic generation. Memetic Retrieval adds evolutionary selection pressure over candidate descriptions, guided by a tool memory that avoids redundant search. On ToolRet (43k tools, 4 domains), FitText improves average retrieval rank from 8.81 to 2.78; on StableToolBench (16,464 APIs), it achieves a 0.73 average pass rate--a 24-point absolute gain over static query retrieval. The gains transfer across base models capable of acting as competent semantic operators; under weaker base models, Memetic's evolutionary search inverts--amplifying noise rather than refining signal--surfacing model capacity as a prerequisite for evolutionary tool exploration.
2605.02411.paper_summary_zh: 語意差距將用戶描述任務的方式與工具文檔的方式分開。隨著 API 生態系統擴展到數萬個端點，僅依賴初始查詢的靜態檢索無法彌補這一差距：代理對其所需內容的理解在執行過程中不斷演變，但其工具集並未隨之改變。我們介紹了 FitText，一個無需訓練的框架，通過將檢索直接嵌入代理的推理循環中，使檢索變得動態。FitText 生成自然語言的偽工具描述作為檢索探針，並通過檢索反饋進行迭代精煉，通過隨機生成探索多樣的替代方案。模因檢索對候選描述施加進化選擇壓力，並通過一個避免冗餘搜索的工具記憶進行指導。在 ToolRet（43,000 個工具，4 個領域）上，FitText 將平均檢索排名從 8.81 提升至 2.78；在 StableToolBench（16,464 個 API）上，它實現了 0.73 的平均通過率——比靜態查詢檢索提高了 24 個絕對點。這些增益在能夠作為有能力的語意操作員的基礎模型之間轉移；在較弱的基礎模型下，模因的進化搜索會反轉——放大噪音而不是精煉信號——將模型能力顯示為進化工具探索的先決條件。
2605.02411.author: Kyle Zheng et.al.
2605.02411.authors: Kyle Zheng, Han Zhang, Renliang Sun, Chenchen Ye, Wei Wang
